name: Claude Code
description: Anthropic's terminal-first agentic coding tool; reference CLI agent

developer.comment: Built by Anthropic, the maker of the Claude models.

first-release.comment: Released as a research preview in Feb 2025; reached general availability (v1.0.0) in May 2025 alongside Claude 4. Using the first public release per the guideline.

status.comment: Actively developed and GA; ships frequent updates across terminal, IDE, desktop, and web. Checked 2026-06-02.

license.comment: The Claude Code agent itself is proprietary/closed source; only the separate Agent SDK is published. License of the agent, not the model.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent for building features, fixing bugs, tests, refactors; not optimized for a single niche.

interface.comment: Ships as a terminal CLI, a VS Code and JetBrains extension, a standalone desktop app, and a browser/web version (claude.ai/code).

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal, as VS Code/Cursor and JetBrains plugins, and in the browser at claude.ai/code; also a standalone desktop app.

open-source.comment: The Claude Code agent is proprietary. The Agent SDK is separately available but the agent's own source is not openly licensed.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Primary shape is a CLI tool; also ships as an IDE extension and runs as a hosted cloud/web service for long-running tasks.

model-choice.comment: User can pick among Claude model tiers (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) but cannot switch to other providers' models from within the agent; only Claude models are supported.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Supports Anthropic Console API key (pay-as-you-go) and own cloud accounts via Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry.

local-model-support.comment: No documented support for local/self-hosted models (Ollama, vLLM, etc.); third-party options are cloud providers serving Claude, plus optional LLM-gateway proxying of Anthropic traffic.

multi-file-editing.comment: Plans and applies coordinated edits across multiple files in a single task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Chains edit -> run -> fix loops end-to-end (e.g. 'write tests, run them, and fix any failures') with minimal per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands, builds, and tests as part of its loop via a Bash tool.

mcp-support.comment: Native Model Context Protocol support for external tools/data sources (Jira, Slack, Google Drive, custom servers).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Spawns multiple agents/subagents that work in parallel with a lead agent coordinating; also background agents for parallel full sessions.

codebase-indexing.comment: Reads files on demand via agentic search rather than building a persistent semantic/embeddings index of the repo.

planning-mode.comment: Has an explicit plan-then-execute flow; plan review is surfaced in the VS Code extension and the CLI plan mode.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image input such as screenshots and mockups, leveraging the underlying Claude models' vision capability.

browser-computer-use.comment: Chrome integration lets it drive and debug live web applications, beyond plain URL fetching.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Model-driven delegation: docs state 'When Claude encounters a task that matches a subagent's description, it delegates to that subagent' and 'Claude uses each subagent's description to decide when to delegate.' Claude picks subagents on its own initiative (and 'use proactively' in a description encourages this), not only via explicit user invocation. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational edits, supervised multi-step runs with per-action approval, autonomous task completion, and async/background runs via web and background agents. No tab inline-completion.

inline-completion.comment: Chat/agent-driven; does not provide as-you-type tab autocomplete suggestions.

background-async-execution.comment: Long-running tasks can run on the web/cloud and as background agents while the developer does other work.

persistent-memory.comment: CLAUDE.md project memory plus auto memory that saves learnings (build commands, debugging insights) across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Built-in /rewind checkpoints (since v2.0.0) snapshot edited files automatically and restore code/conversation/both without git.

git-integration.comment: Works directly with git: stages changes, writes commit messages, creates branches.

pr-creation.comment: Opens pull requests directly and via GitHub Actions / GitLab CI integrations.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Reads CLAUDE.md instruction files at project, repo, and org levels at the start of every session.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Hooks run shell commands before/after actions; plus custom skills/commands and the Agent SDK for programmatic extension.

permission-model.comment: Asks approval per action by default, is configurable (managed permissions, allow/deny rules), and offers a sandboxing mode (introduced Oct 2025).

self-host-on-prem.comment: Cannot be deployed fully on-prem; the agent calls Anthropic-hosted or cloud-provider (Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry) model endpoints. Corporate proxy/LLM-gateway routing is supported but the models remain remote.

privacy-mode.comment: Commercial API, Team, and Enterprise tiers do not use inputs/outputs to train models by default; consumer (Free/Pro/Max) usage offers an opt-out toggle. Checked 2026-06-02.

enterprise-controls.comment: Claude for Enterprise adds SSO/domain capture, role-based permissions, and compliance/audit (compliance API) controls; managed policy settings org-wide.

pricing-model.comment: Included with Pro/Max subscriptions; also usable pay-as-you-go via an Anthropic Console API account. No genuine free tier. Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: No free tier; requires a paid Pro/Max subscription or pay-as-you-go API billing.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan including Claude Code is Pro at $20/mo (US), ~$17/mo if billed annually. Checked 2026-06-02.

maturity.comment: GA since May 2025, widely deployed and polished across terminal/IDE/desktop/web; reference CLI agent with very large adoption.

community-size.comment: Per the guide's thresholds, Claude Code sits at the 'large' tier; widely adopted with a very active developer community.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Meaningful ecosystem of MCP servers, shareable skills/commands, hooks, and the Agent SDK; integrations across GitHub, GitLab, Slack, Chrome.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads CLAUDE.md (and CLAUDE.local.md) at session start; reads CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md, though an AGENTS.md can be pulled in via an @AGENTS.md import or a symlink. Checked 2026-06-02.

memory-scopes.comment: CLAUDE.md scopes span managed policy (org/enterprise), user (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md), project (./CLAUDE.md), and per-folder nested CLAUDE.md files loaded on demand in subdirectories.

scoped-rules-files.comment: `.claude/rules/*.md` support glob `paths:` frontmatter so a rule loads only when Claude touches matching files (path-based, incl. extension globs like **/*.ts); rules without a paths field load always-on. Agent-requested on-demand material is handled by Skills, not rules.

auto-memory.comment: Auto memory (v2.1.59+) writes cross-session notes itself into ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/; on by default and disableable via the /memory toggle, autoMemoryEnabled, or CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY=1.

skill-support.comment: Packaged on-demand Skills (SKILL.md) plus custom slash commands; the skill body loads only when used. Follows the Agent Skills open standard.

skill-invocation.comment: By default both: the user invokes via /skill-name and Claude loads a skill automatically when relevant. `disable-model-invocation` makes it user-only; `user-invocable: false` makes it model-only.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: each skill's description stays in context so Claude knows what's available, while the full SKILL.md body loads only when the skill is invoked.

skill-distribution.comment: Installed by dropping files into .claude/skills/ (manual), shared via git clone/symlink, or bundled in plugins distributed through plugin marketplaces.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Built-in sandboxed Bash tool uses kernel-level OS sandboxing: macOS Seatbelt, Linux/WSL2 bubblewrap plus an optional seccomp filter. Anthropic also officially documents running the whole process in a dev/custom Docker container, a VM/microVM, or Anthropic-managed VMs (Claude Code on the web). Native Windows unsupported (use WSL2). Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Rules take the form Tool or Tool(specifier): bare tool name (per-tool), exact command e.g. Bash(npm run build) (per-command), glob/prefix e.g. Bash(npm run *) (command-prefix), Read/Edit gitignore-style path patterns (per-path), WebFetch(domain:...) and sandbox allowed/deniedDomains (per-domain). Reads run without prompt while writes/commands prompt by default (read/write distinction).

rule-precedence.comment: Docs: 'Rules are evaluated in order: deny, then ask, then allow ... rule specificity does not change the order.' Deny from any settings scope always wins and a more-specific allow cannot carve an exception back out, so the declarative engine tops out at deny-overrides (no gitignore-style nested re-override or user-ordered evaluation). Programmable precedence via PreToolUse hooks is scored under Permission Configurability, not here.

permission-configurability.comment: Highest level reached: beyond presets (permission modes) and user-authored allow/ask/deny declarative rules in settings.json, PreToolUse / PermissionRequest hooks are a code-level decision point that inspects each concrete tool call and can allow, deny, or force a prompt.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No shipped default deny rule for secrets. The built-in protected-path list guards repo/tooling state (.git, .claude, shell rc files, .npmrc, .mcp.json, etc.) but not .env or key material; docs note the sandbox's default read policy still allows reading ~/.aws/credentials and ~/.ssh unless the user adds denyRead. Protection of secrets requires the user to author a deny rule, so false per the guideline. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: 'Yes, don't ask again' for a Bash command saves an allow rule permanently per project directory and command (written to settings.json), so it survives restarts. Manageable via /permissions. File-edit approvals are session-only, but command allowlisting persists.

unsupervised-mode.comment: bypassPermissions mode, entered via --dangerously-skip-permissions (or --permission-mode bypassPermissions), skips all prompts except explicit ask rules and rm -rf / | ~ circuit breakers. Also a research-preview 'auto' mode that suppresses routine prompts behind a safety classifier. Admins can lock either off via disableBypassPermissionsMode / disableAutoMode.

network-access-control.comment: With the Bash sandbox enabled, egress goes through an out-of-sandbox proxy: no domains pre-allowed, first use of a new domain prompts, and allowedDomains/deniedDomains define an allowlist; managed allowManagedDomainsOnly blocks non-allowed hosts outright. Custom corporate proxy/gateway supported. Without the sandbox, Bash network is unrestricted, so the policy is fundamentally configurable. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out-of-the-box 'default' mode runs reads without asking but prompts on first use of each editing tool and each Bash command; protected-path writes always prompt. acceptEdits (auto-edit, ask commands) and bypass are opt-in modes, not the default.

auto-accept-mode.comment: acceptEdits mode is the in-session middle rung: press Shift+Tab from Manual mode (status bar shows '⏵⏵ accept edits on') and Claude creates/edits files in the working directory plus common filesystem commands (mkdir, touch, mv, cp, rm, sed) without per-action prompts, while other Bash commands and protected-path writes still prompt. Reversible by cycling Shift+Tab again; distinct from the full bypassPermissions/--dangerously-skip-permissions switch (Unsupervised Mode). Checked 2026-07-17.
